title: Creamy Tomato Tuna Noodles
summary: A flavour-packed 15-minute dinner. We’ve combined a rich tomato-garlic base with tender ramen noodles, canned tuna and a silky egg yolk sauce for a creamy, satisfying dish. Topped with capers, crispy onions and a hint of chilli, it's the perfect easy midweek dinner with minimal effort.

method.description: Finely grate the garlic and thinly slice the chilli. Drain the tuna. Bring a large saucepan of water to the boil.

Set a frying pan over a medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. Once hot, add the tomato purée and garlic. Cook, stirring, until the tomato paste darkens, about 4-6 mins. Transfer to a large mixing bowl and stir in the red wine vinegar.
Once the water is boiling, cook the ramen noodles following pack instructions. Before draining, reserve the noodle water. Rinse the noodles under cold water.

Add the egg yolks to the bowl with the tomato purée mix. Whisk in some of the reserved noodle water until smooth and creamy. Add the noodles and ½ the tuna to the bowl, tossing to coat. If needed, add more hot noodle water to loosen the sauce. Season with salt and black pepper.
Divide the noodles between four bowls. Top with the capers, sliced green chilli, crispy onions and the remaining tuna.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
